root_cause: OCS had operation-level context, cognition, and replay-derived intent evidence but lacked bounded memory and continuity across related replay-visible operations.
runtime_model: OCS memory and continuity consumes explicit OCS context, cognition, replay-derived intent, domain registry, and replay-visible operation history inputs.
The runtime validates replay visibility, artifact hashes, known OCS artifact types, known OCS artifact statuses, and false authority flags.
Inputs are normalized into source references, domains, operation keys, and intent keys.
Memory summarizes source counts, domains, intent keys, operation groups, and bounded memory text.
Continuity records context linkage, operation grouping, domain continuity, and intent continuity.
The memory hash is derived from normalized sources, memory summary, status, authority flags, and failure state.
The continuity hash is derived from memory hash, linkage, operation groups, domain continuity, intent continuity, status, authority flags, and failure state.
Replay reconstruction validates ordering, wrapper hashes, artifact hashes, memory hash continuity, continuity hash continuity, and returned reference continuity.
replay_impact: OCS now has append-only replay-visible memory and continuity evidence.
Replay reconstruction can prove which sources shaped memory and continuity.
Identical replay-visible source history produces the same memory hash and continuity hash.
The runtime does not mutate source replay.
The runtime does not invoke providers, workers, PPP, approval, governance mutation, implementation, or execution.
commit_message: Certify OCS memory and continuity runtime
